question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe in the kitchen?
answer: A pot holder — the heat helper.

question: What do we call the sound at the end of 'log'?
answer: The /g/ sound — the last bump.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe in the sun?
answer: Sunglasses — the eye protectors.

question: What do we call the person who helps us learn about space?
answer: A science teacher — the space guide.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe on a scooter?
answer: A helmet — the head saver.

question: What do we call the shape that looks like a rectangle but 3D?
answer: A rectangular prism — long and boxy.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe when using scissors?
answer: Holding them by the handles — the safe carry rule.

question: What do we call the person who helps us learn to be kind?
answer: A teacher — the kindness coach.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe in the rain?
answer: Rain boots — splash protectors.

question: What do we call the sound in the middle of 'hop'?
answer: The /o/ sound — short and round.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe when climbing?
answer: Using both hands — the climbing rule.

question: What do we call the person who helps us learn about our community?
answer: A social studies teacher — the community guide.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe when carrying things?
answer: Two hands — the steady helpers.

question: What do we call the shape that looks like a circle cut in half?
answer: A semicircle — half a circle.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe when eating?
answer: Chewing slowly — the safe eating rule.

question: What do we call the person who helps us learn to listen?
answer: A teacher — the listening guide.

question: What do we call the thing that helps us stay safe when playing tag?
answer: Tagging gently — the soft-touch rule.

question: How do you get a value from a Map variable in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Find node. Drag off your Map variable and search for Find. Plug the Key into the Key pin and it returns the Value.

question: How do you set a value in a Map variable in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Add node on the Map. Plug in the Key and Value pins. If the key already exists it will overwrite the value.

question: How do you get a single row from a Data Table in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Get Data Table Row node. Set the Data Table asset on the node and plug a Name into the Row Name pin. It returns the Out Row struct.

question: How do you read a field from a Data Table row?
answer: After Get Data Table Row drag off the Out Row pin and search for Break followed by your struct name. This exposes all the fields.

question: How do you get all row names from a Data Table?
answer: Use the Get Data Table Row Names node. Set the Table pin to your Data Table asset. It returns an array of Names.

question: How do you loop through all rows in a Data Table?
answer: Use Get Data Table Row Names to get the array then feed it into a For Each Loop. Inside the loop use Get Data Table Row with the Array Element as the Row Name.

question: How do you get a value from a Struct variable?
answer: Drag off the struct variable pin and search for Break followed by the struct name. This exposes all the individual fields.

question: How do you set a value inside a Struct variable?
answer: Drag off the struct variable and search for Set members in followed by the struct name. You can then set individual fields.

question: How do you check if a key exists in a Map?
answer: Use the Contains node on the Map. Plug the Key in and it returns a boolean true if the key exists.

question: How do you remove a key from a Map?
answer: Use the Remove node on the Map. Plug the Key in and it will remove that entry from the Map.

question: How do you get the number of entries in a Map?
answer: Use the Length node on the Map variable. It returns an integer count of all key value pairs.

question: How do you initialize a Map at game start?
answer: Use Event Begin Play and loop through your data source adding each Key and Value using the Add node on the Map.

question: What is the difference between a pure function and a regular function in Blueprint?
answer: A pure function has no execution pins and can be called anywhere without breaking the execution chain. A regular function has execution pins and must be wired into the execution flow.

question: How do you create a custom event dispatcher in Blueprint?
answer: In the My Blueprint panel find Event Dispatchers and click the plus button. Name it and add input parameters in the details panel.

question: How do you call an event dispatcher in Blueprint?
answer: Drag the dispatcher from the My Blueprint panel into the graph and select Call. Wire it into your execution chain and fill in the input pins.

question: How do you bind to an event dispatcher in Blueprint?
answer: Drag the dispatcher into the graph and select Bind. Connect the Event pin to a custom event node that will run when the dispatcher fires.

question: How do you get a component from an actor in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Get Component by Class node. Feed in the actor reference and select your component class from the dropdown.

question: How do you get the Player Controller in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Get Player Controller node. Pass in 0 for Player Index to get the first player.

question: How do you get the Player Character in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Get Player Character node. Pass in 0 for Player Index to get the first player character.

question: How do you increment an integer by 1 in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Increment Int node. Drag off your integer variable and search for the double plus symbol or Increment.

question: How do you clamp a float value in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Clamp Float node. Plug your value into Value and set the Min and Max pins.

question: How do you subtract two floats in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Float minus Float node. Drag off any float pin and type the minus symbol or search subtract.

question: How do you check if one float is greater than or equal to another?
answer: Use the Float >= Float node. Returns true if the first value is greater than or equal to the second.

question: How do you check if an integer is greater than or equal to another?
answer: Use the Integer >= Integer node. Returns true if the first value is greater than or equal to the second.

question: How do you divide two floats to get a percentage in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Float divided by Float node. For example CurrentXP divided by MaxXP gives a 0 to 1 value usable in a progress bar.

question: How do you play a sound in Blueprint without a location?
answer: Use the Play Sound 2D node. Plug a Sound Cue or Sound Wave asset into the Sound pin.

question: How do you add a component to a Blueprint class?
answer: Open the Blueprint and in the Components panel at the top left click Add and search for your component class.

question: How do you make a function pure in Blueprint?
answer: Select the function in the My Blueprint panel. In the Details panel on the right check the Pure checkbox.

question: How do you add inputs to a function in Blueprint?
answer: Click on the function entry node in the graph. In the Details panel click the plus button next to Inputs and set the name and type.

question: How do you add outputs to a function in Blueprint?
answer: Click on the function entry node or return node. In the Details panel click the plus button next to Outputs and set the name and type.

question: How do you create a local variable inside a function in Blueprint?
answer: Click on the function entry node. In the Details panel under Local Variables click the plus button and set the name and type.

question: How do you promote a value to a variable in Blueprint?
answer: Right click on any output pin and select Promote to Variable. It creates a new variable and wires it automatically.

question: How do you print a value to the screen for debugging in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Print String node. Convert your value to a string first using the To String conversion node then plug it into the In String pin.

question: How do you convert a float to a string in Blueprint?
answer: Drag off any float pin and search for To String Float. It returns a string version of the value.

question: How do you convert an integer to a string in Blueprint?
answer: Drag off any integer pin and search for To String Integer. It returns a string version of the value.

question: How do you use a Branch node in Blueprint?
answer: The Branch node is like an if statement. Plug a boolean into the Condition pin. True and False are separate execution paths.

question: How do you get a boolean result from a comparison in Blueprint?
answer: Use any comparison node like >= or == and wire the result boolean into a Branch node Condition pin.

question: How do you find the remainder after a subtraction in Blueprint?
answer: Use Float minus Float. For example CurrentXP minus MaxXP gives you the leftover XP after a level up.

question: What node do you use to set a Map value back after modifying it?
answer: Use the Add node on the Map with the same Key. If the key exists it overwrites the old value with the new one.

question: How do you access a Data Table asset reference in Blueprint?
answer: Create a variable of type Data Table Object Reference. In the Details panel set the default value to your Data Table asset.

question: How do you loop through an array in Blueprint?
answer: Feed the array into a For Each Loop node. The Array Element pin gives you each item and Array Index gives the position.

question: How do you get the length of an array in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Length node on the array. It returns an integer of how many items are in the array.

question: How do you add an item to an array in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Add node on the array variable. Plug the item into the New Item pin.

question: How do you clear all entries from a Map in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Clear node on the Map variable. This removes all key value pairs from the Map.

question: How do you use Event Begin Play in a component Blueprint?
answer: In the Event Graph right click and search Event Begin Play. This fires when the component initializes and is used to set up default values.

question: How do you duplicate a function in Blueprint?
answer: Right click the function name in the My Blueprint panel and select Duplicate. Rename the copy in the Details panel.

question: How do you make a Map with a Name key and Float value in Blueprint?
answer: Create a new variable. Set the type to Map. Set the Key type to Name and the Value type to Float.

question: How do you make a Map with a Name key and Integer value in Blueprint?
answer: Create a new variable. Set the type to Map. Set the Key type to Name and the Value type to Integer.

question: How do you wire execution pins in Blueprint?
answer: Drag from the white triangle output pin on one node to the white triangle input pin on the next node to create an execution connection.

question: How do you re-route a wire in Blueprint to keep it tidy?
answer: Double click on any wire to add a reroute node. This lets you bend wires around other nodes to keep the graph readable.

question: How do you get a struct field without breaking the whole struct?
answer: Drag off the struct pin and search for the field name directly. UE5 often lets you access individual members without a full Break node.

question: How do you check if a Data Table row was found successfully?
answer: The Get Data Table Row node has a Row Found and Row Not Found execution pin. Wire your logic off Row Found to ensure the row exists before using it.

question: How do you set a default value for a variable in Blueprint?
answer: Compile the Blueprint first. Then select the variable in the My Blueprint panel and in the Details panel set the Default Value field.

question: How do you call a function from another Blueprint in UE5?
answer: Get a reference to the other Blueprint first using Get Component by Class or a cast. Then drag off the reference and search for the function name.

question: How do you use a Select node in Blueprint?
answer: The Select node returns different values based on an index or boolean input. Wire a boolean into the Index pin and set the A and B pins for false and true outputs.

question: How do you convert a Name to a String in Blueprint?
answer: Drag off any Name pin and search for Name To String. It returns a readable string version of the Name value.

question: How do you convert a String to a Name in Blueprint?
answer: Drag off any String pin and search for String To Name. It returns a Name type usable as a Map key or row name.

question: How do you use a Format Text node in Blueprint?
answer: Right click and search Format Text. In the Format field type your text with variables in curly braces like Level: {0}. Wire your values into the numbered pins.

question: How do you get a random integer between two values in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Random Integer in Range node. Set the Min and Max pins to define the range.

question: How do you get a random float between two values in Blueprint?
answer: Use the Random Float in Range node. Set the Min and Max pins to define the range.
